quiz.title: Unit 7 Quiz 4, Biology I
quiz.subtitle: Cell Division: Mitosis Details, Checkpoints, and Structures
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Prometaphase, Kinetochore Function, Spindle Assembly Checkpoint, Cohesin Cleavage, Cell Plate, Chromatid Count

questions.question: Which phase is sometimes very short or skipped in rapidly dividing cells?
questions.explanation: In rapidly dividing cells (like embryonic cells), G1 phase is often very short or skipped entirely because cells grow quickly and need to divide rapidly. These cells may also spend less time in G2, but G1 is the most variable phase.

questions.question: What is the role of the kinetochore during mitosis?
questions.explanation: The kinetochore is a protein structure that forms at the centromere of each sister chromatid. It serves as the attachment site for spindle microtubules, allowing chromosomes to be moved during anaphase.

questions.question: Which phase of mitosis is characterized by the disappearance of the nuclear envelope?
questions.explanation: Prometaphase is marked by the complete breakdown of the nuclear envelope into vesicles, allowing spindle fibers to access and attach to chromosomes. (Some textbooks include this as part of prophase, but prometaphase is the specific stage.)

questions.question: What happens to the nucleolus during prophase?
options: It moves to the metaphase plate
questions.explanation: The nucleolus, where rRNA is transcribed and ribosomes are assembled, disappears during prophase as chromatin condenses and transcription ceases. It reforms in telophase.

questions.question: During metaphase, what ensures that chromosomes are correctly aligned?
questions.explanation: The spindle assembly checkpoint (SAC) ensures all chromosomes are properly attached to spindle fibers at the metaphase plate before anaphase begins. This prevents chromosome mis-segregation.

questions.question: In anaphase, sister chromatids separate because:
questions.explanation: Sister chromatids are held together by cohesin protein complexes. At anaphase onset, the enzyme separase cleaves cohesin, allowing chromatids to be pulled apart by spindle fibers.

questions.question: During telophase, which of the following occurs?
questions.explanation: In telophase, nuclear envelopes reform around each set of daughter chromosomes, the nucleolus reappears, and chromosomes begin to decondense back into chromatin.

questions.question: Which structure is unique to plant cell mitosis during cytokinesis?
questions.explanation: Plant cells form a cell plate from Golgi-derived vesicles that fuse at the metaphase plate, eventually becoming the new cell wall. Animal cells use a cleavage furrow and contractile ring.

questions.question: Which statement about the mitotic spindle is correct?
options: It is made of actin filaments
It is permanent in the cell
questions.explanation: The mitotic spindle, made of microtubules, guides chromosome movement by attaching to kinetochores and pulling chromatids apart. It forms during prophase and disassembles after telophase.

questions.question: A cell with 8 chromosomes enters mitosis. How many chromatids are present during metaphase?
questions.explanation: After S phase, each chromosome consists of two sister chromatids. So 8 chromosomes × 2 chromatids each = 16 chromatids present during metaphase. After anaphase separation, each daughter cell will have 8 chromosomes (now single chromatids).

questions.question: Which of the following is a key difference between prophase and prometaphase?
options: Nuclear envelope breakdown occurs in prometaphase
questions.explanation: In prophase, chromosomes condense but the nuclear envelope is still intact. In prometaphase, the nuclear envelope breaks down completely, allowing spindle fibers to attach to chromosomes.

questions.question: During mitosis, the centrioles move toward:
questions.explanation: Centrioles duplicate during interphase and move to opposite poles of the cell during prophase and prometaphase, where they organize the spindle fibers.

questions.question: Which checkpoint prevents anaphase from occurring if chromosomes are not properly attached to the spindle?
questions.explanation: The spindle assembly checkpoint (M checkpoint) occurs at metaphase and ensures all chromosomes are correctly attached to spindle fibers before anaphase begins.

questions.question: The mitotic phase of the cell cycle ensures:
options: Production of genetically unique daughter cells
questions.explanation: Mitosis ensures that daughter cells have the same chromosome number as the parent cell (maintenance of chromosome number). Meiosis halves chromosome number for gamete production.

questions.question: Which of the following events occurs during early prophase?
questions.explanation: During early prophase, chromatin condenses into visible chromosomes. Sister chromatids separate in anaphase, nuclear envelope reforms in telophase, and cytokinesis occurs after mitosis.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered the finer details of mitosis! 🔬
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the spindle assembly checkpoint and kinetochore function.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on chromosome behavior during each mitotic stage.
scoreMessages.0: Please review prometaphase, cohesin cleavage, and chromosome counts.
